title: Slavery in the British colonies
description: Practice your historical skills in this quiz on slavery in the British colonies.

assessment_items.question_content:
Analyze the graph and answer the question below. 

**Enslaved laborers transported to the Western Hemisphere, 1450-1900**
[[☃ image 1]]

**The overall trend from 1601 to 1800 depicted in the graph resulted from which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

options.alt: Bar graph showing number of enslaved migrants transported to the Western Hemisphere by century. Between 1450 and 1600, 367,000 enslaved migrants were transported, between 1601 and 1700, 1,868,000 enslaved migrants were transported, between 1701 and 1800, 6,133,000 enslaved migrants were transported, and between 1801 and 1900, 3,330,000 enslaved migrants were transported.

choices.content: the growth of plantation agriculture in the Americas
the emergence of an international anti-slavery movement
the decrease in available farmland in West African countries
the decline in the population of Europe following the Plague

assessment_items.question_content:
Analyze the graph and answer the question below. 

**Enslaved laborers transported to the Western Hemisphere, 1450-1900**
[[☃ image 1]]

**The table above could support which of the following arguments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Bar graph showing number of enslaved migrants transported to the Western Hemisphere by century. Between 1450 and 1600, 367,000 enslaved migrants were transported, between 1601 and 1700, 1,868,000 enslaved migrants were transported, between 1701 and 1800, 6,133,000 enslaved migrants were transported, and between 1801 and 1900, 3,330,000 enslaved migrants were transported.

choices.content: After Bacon’s Rebellion, there was an increase in the Atlantic slave trade as enslaved Africans replaced indentured servants.
After Metacom’s War, there was an increase in the Atlantic slave trade as enslaved Africans replaced indigenous labor.
After the founding of Jamestown, there was a decrease in the Atlantic slave trade as indentured servants replaced enslaved Africans as the main source of labor.
After the Stono Rebellion, there was a decrease in the Atlantic slave trade as British colonists started using indigenous labor.

assessment_items.question_content:
Analyze the graph and answer the question below. 

**Enslaved laborers transported to the Western Hemisphere, 1450-1900**
[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following best explains the change in the population of enslaved Africans from 1601 to 1800?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Bar graph showing number of enslaved migrants transported to the Western Hemisphere by century. Between 1450 and 1600, 367,000 enslaved migrants were transported, between 1601 and 1700, 1,868,000 enslaved migrants were transported, between 1701 and 1800, 6,133,000 enslaved migrants were transported, and between 1801 and 1900, 3,330,000 enslaved migrants were transported.

choices.content: Enslaved Africans performed agricultural labor in the West Indies and the Southern and Chesapeake colonies.
Enslaved Africans performed agricultural labor in the New England and Middle colonies.
Enslaved Africans worked in shipbuilding in the New England and Middle colonies.
Enslaved Africans worked in the fishing industry in major port cities along the Atlantic coast.

assessment_items.question_content:
Analyze the graph and answer the question below. 

**Enslaved laborers transported to the Western Hemisphere, 1450-1900**
[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following groups would have advocated for the increase in enslaved Africans as depicted in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Bar graph showing number of enslaved migrants transported to the Western Hemisphere by century. Between 1450 and 1600, 367,000 enslaved migrants were transported, between 1601 and 1700, 1,868,000 enslaved migrants were transported, between 1701 and 1800, 6,133,000 enslaved migrants were transported, and between 1801 and 1900, 3,330,000 enslaved migrants were transported.

choices.content: plantation owners in the West Indies
New England Puritans
Southern indentured servants
German immigrants who settled in Pennsylvania

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question below. 

> “Whereas some doubts have arisen whether children produced by any Englishman and a negro woman should be slave or free, be it therefore enacted and declared by this present grand assembly, that all children borne in this country shall be held bond, or free only according to the condition of the mother. And if any Christian shall commit fornication with a negro man or woman, he or she so offending shall pay double the fines imposed by the former act.”

>-Source: Virginia slave law passed by the House of Burgesses in December 1662

**Which of the following was a long-term result of the excerpt above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the prohibition of interracial relationships under strict colonial laws
the rise of an abolition movement in the southern colonies
the guarantee of freedom for descendants of interracial relationships
the end of enslaving African children born in the colonies

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question below. 

> “Whereas some doubts have arisen whether children produced by any Englishman and a negro woman should be slave or free, be it therefore enacted and declared by this present grand assembly, that all children borne in this country shall be held bond, or free only according to the condition of the mother. And if any Christian shall commit fornication with a negro man or woman, he or she so offending shall pay double the fines imposed by the former act.”

>-Source: Virginia slave law passed by the House of Burgesses in December 1662

**Which of the following developments in the colonial era best represented the continuation of the ideas expressed in the slave law above?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the idea that the condition of slavery passed down to the child through the mother
the idea that slave owners have the right to recapture enslaved Africans if they run away
the idea that enslaved people of African descent are not considered citizens in the eyes of the law
the idea that terms of enslavement only lasted seven years or until a person could buy their freedom

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question below. 

> “Whereas some doubts have arisen whether children produced by any Englishman and a negro woman should be slave or free, be it therefore enacted and declared by this present grand assembly, that all children borne in this country shall be held bond, or free only according to the condition of the mother. And if any Christian shall commit fornication with a negro man or woman, he or she so offending shall pay double the fines imposed by the former act.”

>-Source: Virginia slave law passed by the House of Burgesses in December 1662

**The excerpt would be most useful to historians as evidence for which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the relationships between British colonists and enslaved Africans in the seventeenth and eighteenth centuries
the rise in the number of rebellions led by enslaved people in the seventeenth and eighteenth centuries
the role that representative governments had in enforcing the Navigation Acts in the seventeenth century
the economic differences between the different regions of colonies in the seventeenth century

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question below. 

> “Whereas some doubts have arisen whether children produced by any Englishman and a negro woman should be slave or free, be it therefore enacted and declared by this present grand assembly, that all children borne in this country shall be held bond, or free only according to the condition of the mother. And if any Christian shall commit fornication with a negro man or woman, he or she so offending shall pay double the fines imposed by the former act.”

>-Source: Virginia slave law passed by the House of Burgesses in December 1662

**Which of the following describes the change highlighted in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Children inherited enslaved status through their mother’s line for life.
Enslaved people of African descent received legal protections after they converted to Christianity.
Slave owners kept enslaved families together and refused to separate children from their mothers.
Colonial law determined that a child of a British colonist and an enslaved person of African descent was free.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question below. 

> “I reckon it was hot, ‘cause in less than two days, 21 white men, women, and children, and 44 Negroes, was slain. My granddaddy say that in the woods and at Stono, where the war start, there was more than 100 Negroes in line. When the militia come in sight of them at Combahee swamp, the drinking, dancing Negroes scatter in the brush and only 44 stand their ground. Commander Cato speak for the crowd. He say: ‘We don’t like slavery. We start to join the Spanish in Florida. We surrender but we not whipped yet and we is not converted.’ The other 43 say: ‘Amen.’ They was taken, unarmed, and hanged by the militia.”

>-Source: George Cato, account of the Stono Rebellion in 1739

**Which of the following best represents continuity in the years after 1739 with the ideas that the author expressed in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the raid on Harpers Ferry, when a group of white and black men attempted to start an armed rebellion
the battle of New Orleans, when a small American force routed a much larger British force during the War of 1812
the convention at Seneca Falls, when women’s rights activists came together to draft the Declaration of Sentiments
the San Francisco riot, when the white population of the city attacked Chinese immigrants in 1877

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question below. 

> “I reckon it was hot, ‘cause in less than two days, 21 white men, women, and children, and 44 Negroes, was slain. My granddaddy say that in the woods and at Stono, where the war start, there was more than 100 Negroes in line. When the militia come in sight of them at Combahee swamp, the drinking, dancing Negroes scatter in the brush and only 44 stand their ground. Commander Cato speak for the crowd. He say: ‘We don’t like slavery. We start to join the Spanish in Florida. We surrender but we not whipped yet and we is not converted.’ The other 43 say: ‘Amen.’ They was taken, unarmed, and hanged by the militia.”

>-Source: George Cato, account of the Stono Rebellion in 1739

**A historian would most likely use this passage to illustrate which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ways that enslaved Africans resisted the conditions of slavery
ways that British colonists resisted the British monarchy
ways that indigenous nations resisted colonial encroachment
ways that enslaved Africans resisted converting to Christianity

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question below. 

> “I reckon it was hot, ‘cause in less than two days, 21 white men, women, and children, and 44 Negroes, was slain. My granddaddy say that in the woods and at Stono, where the war start, there was more than 100 Negroes in line. When the militia come in sight of them at Combahee swamp, the drinking, dancing Negroes scatter in the brush and only 44 stand their ground. Commander Cato speak for the crowd. He say: ‘We don’t like slavery. We start to join the Spanish in Florida. We surrender but we not whipped yet and we is not converted.’ The other 43 say: ‘Amen.’ They was taken, unarmed, and hanged by the militia.”

>-Source: George Cato, account of the Stono Rebellion in 1739

**Which of the following describes a long-term response by English colonists after Stono Rebellion?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: instituting slave codes to prevent further uprisings
sending all of their enslaved Africans to Spanish Florida
creating limits for the maximum years of enslavement
passing laws to protect enslaved people from physical abuse

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question below. 

> “I reckon it was hot, ‘cause in less than two days, 21 white men, women, and children, and 44 Negroes, was slain. My granddaddy say that in the woods and at Stono, where the war start, there was more than 100 Negroes in line. When the militia come in sight of them at Combahee swamp, the drinking, dancing Negroes scatter in the brush and only 44 stand their ground. Commander Cato speak for the crowd. He say: ‘We don’t like slavery. We start to join the Spanish in Florida. We surrender but we not whipped yet and we is not converted.’ The other 43 say: ‘Amen.’ They was taken, unarmed, and hanged by the militia.”

>-Source: George Cato, account of the Stono Rebellion in 1739

**Which of the following describes a way in which enslaved Africans would resist the conditions of slavery?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: breaking tools
working at a faster pace
converting to Judaism
contracting smallpox
